name: Kabocha Squash Chowder
recipeInstructions:
1. Render the diced bacon until crispy and remove it. Set aside.
2. In a little of the rendered bacon fat, slowly sweat the celery, onion, and peppers with 1/4 teaspoon salt until softened.
3. Add garlic and chopped herbs, cooking until fragrant for about 2 minutes.
4. Stir in flour and cook for another 2 minutes.
5. Gradually add one-third of the stock, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Then add the remaining stock, squash, and bay leaves.
6. Simmer gently until the squash is tender but still holds its shape. The soup will thicken as it cooks.
7. Season with salt and pepper.
8. Remove from heat. Stir in scallions, bacon pieces, and parsley. Garnish with toasted or smoked chopped almonds for a crunchy texture.
